title: (Un)Targeted Metabolomics_DataAnalysis_MTH Tours
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f5amk","text":"This protocol describes how metabolomic and lipidomic data produced at MetaboHUB-Tours are analysed. It follows on from the sample preparation and LC-MS parameter protocols.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v5p2","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"442b2","text":"The result is a list of identified compounds (with a stable signal in the sequence) in the samples, based on a confidence level for the identification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ca3v5","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85jj6","text":"To further interpret the data, software and web page suggestions are provided.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1vd99","text":"For all softwares used in this protocol, the original papers \u0026 the website link are available in the protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2p399","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fq1sd","text":"Remember, on MetaboHUB Tours, every sample is injected 3 times : positive reversed-phase, negative reversed-phase \u0026 positive HILIC so, some steps of the protocols need to be done for each modality.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fae2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9q8vk","text":"This protocols has been used only on LC-MS and LC-MS/MS data.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"djooc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"faoc0","text":"Regarding the storage of your data, if your data is of clinical origin, please remember that it must be anonymised.(NIH recommendations: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9373195/)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":137,"length":49}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5A4C86C900DB11F1BF800A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9373195"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"e2paf","text":"Computer, with at least 4 cores.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epmlk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ng6a","text":"All the softwares required are listed in the protocol, with a link to official website or repository.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: All standards compounds have been injected on the LC-MS system in LC-MS². Standards are coming from the MSMLS, OAMLS,BACSMLS and FAMLS chemical library from IROA Technologies (purchased from Merck).

GNPS libraries relate to clinical context have been downloaded : GNPS-NIH-CLINICALCOLLECTION1, GNPS-NIH-CLINICALCOLLECTION2, GNPS-NIH-SMALLMOLECULEPHARMACOLOGICALLYACTIVE, GNPS-COLLECTIONS-PESTICIDES-POSITIVE, GNPS-COLLECTIONS-PESTICIDES-NEGATIVE, GNPS-NIST14-MATCHES & MONA GNPS Mingxun Wang, Jeremy J Carver, Vanessa V Phelan, Laura M Sanchez, Neha Garg, Yao Peng, Don Duy Nguyen, Jeramie Watrous, Clifford Sharing and community curation of mass spectrometr. Nature Biotechnology.

Features detection and MS² spectra extraction have been carried out on one QC raw files , with MSconvert from ProteoWizard (NB : This step can also be performed on specific QC for subgroups or on different types of samples.) Identification of features from the QC is carried out in two stages, using Flash Entropy : Only with the in-house msp files, generated in step 1.1 --> level of identification = 1 Only with the GNPS-based spectral libraries msp files, generated in step 1.2 --> level of identification = 2 or 3 These identification levels are based on High resolution mass spectrometry for structural . Metabolomics. All MS² spectra comparison and so, identification, is done manually with the parameters : Report top n hits = 5 Precursor m/z tolerance (in Da) = 0.01 Product ions m/z tolerance (in Da) = 0.02 identity_search-score < 0.75 are not checked As an ouput files, you have a csv files with all hits found. Compounds with a identity_search-score < 0.75 are deleted. Identification are sorted based on hits and manual curation on MS² "experimental vs. theoritical" spectra comparison. MSconvert (ProteoWizard) Chambers, M.C., MacLean, B., Burke, R., Amode, D., Ruderman, D.L., Neumann, S., Gatto, L., Fischer, B., Pratt, B., Egertson, J., Flash Entropy Search GitHub Yuanyue Li A cross-platform toolkit for mass spectrometry and. Nature Biotechnology. Flash entropy search to query all mass spectral li. Nature Methods. A csv files with all matching --> you need tu do some curation.
All samples are injected in MS1 mode and converted in mzML format with MSconvert from ProteoWizard .

Data Curation = Signal Normalization & Metabolites Exclusion : - Normalization #1 (if possible) : normalization to the mass, the nomber of cells or others parameters - Normalization #2 :of metabolite areas to the total area of detected metabolites - Calculation of the coefficient of variation (CV) for each metabolite in the QCs (analytical variability) and in the samples (biological variability) - Removal of metabolites with analytical variability > biological variability - Removal of metabolites with analytical variability > 30% Signal Normalization and Metabolites Exclusion are repetead until there is no further exclusion

MetaboAnalyst : pathways & statistical models MetaboAnalyst is a comprehensive web-based platform designed for statistical, functional, and integrative analysis of metabolomics data. It helps researchers interpret complex datasets through visualization tools, pathway analysis, and biomarker discovery. The platform is widely used to translate raw metabolomics data into biological insights. MetaboAnalyst 6.0 Jianguo Xia , Nick Psychogios , Nelson Young , David S. Wishart MetaboAnalyst: a web server for metabolomic data a. Nucleic Acids Research.

MetExplore : metabolic network MetExplore is a web server dedicated to the exploration and analysis of metabolic networks. It allows users to visualize metabolic pathways, study network structures, and integrate omics data into genome-scale metabolic models. This tool supports systems biology approaches to better understand metabolism. MetExplore 3.7.8 MetaboHUB Staff (https://www.metabohub.fr/) Ludovic Cottret , Clément Frainay , Maxime Chazalviel , Floréal Cabanettes , Yoann Gloaguen , Etienne Camenen , Benjamin Merlet , Stéphanie Heux , Jean-Charles Portais , Nathalie Poupin , Florence Vinson , Fabien Jourdan. MetExplore: collaborative edition and exploration . Nucleic Acids Research. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gky301

FORUM : Metabolism Knowledge Network Portal FORUM is a knowledge-sharing portal that connects researchers to curated resources, databases, and tools related to metabolism. It promotes collaboration and data exchange within the metabolism research community. The portal helps integrate dispersed metabolic knowledge into a unified network FORUM Knowledge graph Database beta Maxime Delmas , Olivier Filangi , Nils Paulhe , Florence Vinson , Christophe Duperier , William Garrier , Paul-Emeric Saunier FORUM: building a Knowledge Graph from public data. Bioinformatics.

Features detection and MS² spectra extraction have been carried out on raw files , with Spec2Xtract . Currently, Spect2Xtract does not work with Waters files. Instead, you can use MSconvert (cf step 6) Spec2Xtract GitHub Sylvain Dechaumet

All these repositories have been cured, concatened and redondancies have been sorted with FragHub ; leading to a unique library file in POS and in NEG. Two files, in a msp format, for your spectral library in POS and in NEG. Iy there are in-silico spectrum, they will create another msp files, specific to them.
mzML files are then transfert to Workflow4Metabolomics for analysis. Workflow4Metabolomics 25.0 Galaxy doi: 10.1093/bioinformatics/btu813

Output files are a csv files with all features detected in samples & a folder with a png file of the chromatographic peak corresponding to each features detected At this step, you may have several files because you had multiple injections. In our case, we have 3 files : HILIC +, C18 +, C18 -

MetaboLights : a database for metabolomics experiments and derived information MetaboLights is an open-access repository for metabolomics experiments and their derived information. It enables researchers to store, share, and reuse metabolomics datasets along with standardized metadata. This database supports data transparency, reproducibility, and long-term preservation of metabolomics studies MetaboLights Ozgur Yurekten, Thomas Payne, Noemi Tejera, Felix Xavier Amaladoss, Callum Martin, Mark Williams, Claire O’Donovan Ozgur Yurekten, Thomas Payne, Noemi Tejera, Felix Xavier Amaladoss, Callum Martin, Mark Williams, Claire O’Donovan. MetaboLights: open data repository for metabolomic. Nucleic Acids Research. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkad1045

All these files have been concatened with FragHub ; leading to a unique library file in POS and in NEG. One msp file / ionisation mode. Be careful to not mix different modalities. For example, HILIC + and C18 + files must not be treated at the same time in FragHub because of the difference in retention time. FragHub 1.4.1 GitHub Axel DaBlanc FragHub: A Mass Spectral Library Data Integration . Analytical Chemistry.

Fusion of modalites = sorting redundancies: Only the best modality is kept for 1 metabolite, based on RT > Dead Volume (0-1 min) and/or lower CV(metabolite) on QCs "Analysis Validation" is proceed At this step, you must gather your different files IF you have several ones

Analysis Validation : the analyses were validated by observing the distribution of QCs among the samples using Principal Component Analysis (PCA). For all PCAs, the data were log-transformed and underwent UV scaling normalization. --> Another control of the quality is done with a PCA displaying " blank vs. blank of extraction vs. QC" to check that our identification are not only noises.

RESULTS FILES The final file is a table with the following information: A B C D E F G H I J K L M 1 Best modality kept delta rt delta ppm Name ID KEGG ID HMDB ID CHEBI ID Several MetExplore ID mz rt samples ... QC ... CV(CQ)
